message:
type: SURVEY
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Adding TTL support: Cache.__init__ (ttl param), Cache.put (disk_cache.set with expire), Cache.get (lazy expire() call), configure_cache() (ttl param), new set_ttl() helper, DSPY_CACHE init, __all__ export

type: SURVEY
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Feature: on-disk compression (gzip/zlib) with magic-header b'DSPC' format in Cache.put/get, plus cull_limit/eviction_policy knobs surfaced via configure_cache(). Need to edit Cache.__init__ (add compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy params + FanoutCache init), Cache.put (pickle+compress+header), Cache.get (detect header, decompress), optional Cache.set_compression(). In __init__.py need to edit configure_cache() signature/body and DSPY_CACHE construction.

type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py
note: I will own cache.py and implement both TTL support (mine) and your compression/cull_limit/eviction_policy changes (per your SPEC) there.

type: DEFER
files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
spec: In dspy/clients/__init__.py: (1) Add 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' parameter to configure_cache() signature (after memory_max_entries, before enable_litellm_cache is fine, keep enable_litellm_cache last or wherever - just needs to be a valid optional kwarg), and pass it through to the Cache(...) constructor call as ttl=ttl (Cache constructor in cache.py will accept ttl as a new param positioned after memory_max_entries). (2) Add a new module-level function 'def set_ttl(seconds: Optional[int]) -> None' that does: 'import dspy; dspy.cache.set_ttl(seconds)' (I will add a set_ttl(self, seconds) method on the Cache class in cache.py that updates self.memory_cache's ttl - if enable_memory_cache, replaces self.memory_cache with a new TTLCache(maxsize=<current maxsize>, ttl=seconds) if seconds is not None else LRUCache(maxsize=<current maxsize>), preserving existing entries via .update(); and stores self.default_ttl = seconds for use in put()/get(). So set_ttl in __init__.py just delegates to dspy.cache.set_ttl(seconds)). (3) Update the DSPY_CACHE = Cache(...) call at module load to pass ttl=None explicitly (or just omit it since default is None - either is fine, but explicit is preferred for clarity). (4) Add 'set_ttl' to the __all__ list. Docstring for configure_cache should mention 'ttl: Optional default time-to-live in seconds for cache entries; None means entries never expire.' No changes needed to imports in __init__.py beyond what's already there (Cache is already imported).
note: SPEC for TTL feature - please implement in __init__.py

type: SPEC
files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
spec: Please add to configure_cache(...) these new optional params, and pass them through to the Cache(...) constructor call (and also update the module-level DSPY_CACHE = Cache(...) construction to pass compress=None, cull_limit=None, eviction_policy=None as defaults):
- compress: Optional[str] = None  # one of 'gzip', 'zlib', or None. Docstring: 'Compression codec for on-disk cache entries ("gzip", "zlib", or None to disable).'
- cull_limit: Optional[int] = None  # passed through to Cache() constructor; docstring: 'Number of items culled per eviction pass on the disk cache (maps to diskcache cull_limit). None keeps the diskcache default.'
- eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None  # passed through to Cache() constructor; docstring: 'Eviction policy for the on-disk cache (e.g. "least-recently-stored", "least-recently-used", "least-frequently-used", "none"). None keeps the diskcache default.'
These three become keyword args to  in configure_cache, alongside whatever ttl param you add. I will implement Cache.__init__ in cache.py to accept compress=None, cull_limit=None, eviction_policy=None kwargs (in addition to existing enable_disk_cache, enable_memory_cache, disk_cache_dir, disk_size_limit_bytes, memory_max_entries, and your ttl). No changes needed to __all__ for these three (no new public helper unless you want dspy.cache.set_compression exposed — I'll add that method on the Cache class itself in cache.py, no import changes needed in __init__.py for it).

type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py
note: ACK: received your SPEC for cache.py (compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params in Cache.__init__, plus compress/decompress logic in put/get, optional set_compression method). I will implement both my TTL feature and your spec in cache.py. Please confirm CLAIM on dspy/clients/__init__.py and that you received my SPEC for ttl/set_ttl there.

type: SPEC
files: dspy/clients/cache.py
spec: Resending clean (previous message may have been mangled by shell backticks). Compression feature spec for cache.py, to merge with your TTL work:

1) Cache.__init__ new params (add after memory_max_entries, before ttl or after - order flexible): compress: Optional[str] = None, cull_limit: Optional[int] = None, eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None. Store self.compress = compress. When constructing FanoutCache(shards=16, timeout=10, directory=disk_cache_dir, size_limit=disk_size_limit_bytes), also pass cull_limit=cull_limit if cull_limit is not None, and eviction_policy=eviction_policy if eviction_policy is not None (else let diskcache use its own defaults - do not pass the kwarg at all if None, since FanoutCache defaults differ from None).

2) Add method: def set_compression(self, codec: Optional[str]) -> None: validates codec in (None, "gzip", "zlib") else raise ValueError, then sets self.compress = codec (no need to touch existing entries; only affects future put() calls).

3) In Cache.put(), after computing the value to store on disk: build bytes via pickle.dumps(value) using the pickle module (import pickle at top of file), then if self.compress is not None: compress the pickled bytes with gzip.compress(data) or zlib.compress(data) depending on codec; prepend a 5-byte header: b"DSPC" + one codec byte (b"\\x00" = none, b"\\x01" = gzip, b"\\x02" = zlib); then store header+payload bytes into self.disk_cache[key] = <header+payload bytes> instead of storing the raw Python object directly. IMPORTANT: only do this header/pickle wrapping when self.compress is not None (i.e. when compression is enabled) OR always wrap with header even when compress=None (codec byte 0x00) - your choice, but must be internally consistent with step 4 read path. Recommended: only apply the magic-header wrapping when self.compress is truthy (not None), so default (no compression configured) behavior is completely unchanged (backward compatible, stores raw object like today). When self.compress is set, wrap as described.

4) In Cache.get(), when reading self.disk_cache[key], the raw stored value could be: (a) a plain Python object (today's legacy behavior / compress=None never enabled), or (b) bytes starting with the 4-byte magic b"DSPC" followed by 1 codec byte then payload (when compression was used). So: after fetching raw = self.disk_cache[key], check: if isinstance(raw, (bytes, bytearray)) and raw[:4] == b"DSPC": codec_byte = raw[4]; payload = raw[5:]; if codec_byte == 1: payload = gzip.decompress(payload); elif codec_byte == 2: payload = zlib.decompress(payload); elif codec_byte == 0: pass (no-op); response = pickle.loads(payload). Else (no magic header, or not bytes): response = raw as-is (existing behavior, handles legacy entries and any case where compression was never enabled). This must happen BEFORE the existing "add to memory cache" logic so memory cache gets the decompressed Python object, not raw bytes.

5) Imports to add at top of cache.py: import gzip, import pickle, import zlib (alongside existing imports).

6) Backward compat requirement: any disk entry written before this feature (plain pickled object stored via diskcache directly, no wrapping) must still load correctly - covered by the isinstance+magic-header check in step 4, since legacy entries will not be bytes starting with b"DSPC" (they are arbitrary Python objects, diskcache handles their own pickling internally).

Let me know if you want me to just write this part myself as a patch you apply, since it is fiddly byte-level code - happy to hand you exact code if easier.

type: CLAIM

I will implement that plus my compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params in configure_cache(). Claiming ownership of dspy/clients/__init__.py.

Still waiting on your CLAIM ack for dspy/clients/__init__.py (my ttl/set_ttl SPEC) before I start editing - please confirm.

type: DONE
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: IMPORTANT: our sandboxes appear to be isolated, not a shared filesystem - my cache.py edits never appeared in your view, and your __init__.py edits never appeared in mine. Each agent's patch.txt is evaluated independently and must be self-contained. I've now implemented BOTH cache.py (TTL + your compression spec) AND my own minimal ttl-only edits to __init__.py (configure_cache ttl param, set_ttl() helper, DSPY_CACHE ttl=None, __all__) in my own sandbox so my TTL patch is complete and correct on its own. You should do the same for your compression feature: make sure your own patch.txt has all files your feature needs, including cache.py changes, since mine won't transfer to your submission. Sorry for the confusion - good luck!
